LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER BENNETT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA FOSS TO ACKNOWLEDGE PARTIAL SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 724.110 IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. FIRST, THE MOVING PARTY FAILS TO PRESENT COMPETENT EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING EXACTLY HOW MUCH MONEY WAS PAID TO PARTIALLY SATISFY THE JUDGMENT. SECOND, DEFENDANT APPEALED AN ORDER DATED JUNE 23, 2015, AND THE COURT IS UNCERTAIN REGARDING WHICH JUNE 23, 2015, ORDER WAS APPEALED AND WHETHER THAT APPEAL AFFECTS THIS MOTION. IF THE APPEAL IS RELATED TO THIS MOTION THEN THE COURT LACKS JURISDICTION PER CCP SECTION 916(A). THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Renewed Motion To Compel Plaintiff Cynthia Foss To Acknowledge Partial Satisfaction Of Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 724.110
Off Calendar Mar-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT PETER BENNETT's Renewed Motion To Compel Plaintiff Cynthia Foss To Acknowledge Partial Satisfaction Of Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 724.110, Defendant Peter Bennett's motion to compel the acknowledgement of a partial satisfaction of judgment is granted. Per CCP 724.110(b), plaintiff must provide defendant with an acknowledgement of partial satisfaction of judgment in the amount of $351,376.80. Defendant has addressed the concerns underlying the denial of his prior motion. The appeal in this case has been dismissed and defendant has provided sufficient evidence of the amounts transferred to plaintiff. The disentitlement doctrine does not apply in this court. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Motion To Compel Plaintiff Cynthia Foss To Acknowledge Partial Satisfaction Of Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 724.110
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PETER BENNETT'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Cynthia Foss To Acknowledge Partial Satisfaction Of Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 724.110 is denied without prejudice. First, the moving party fails to present competent evidence demonstrating exactly how much money was paid to partially satisfy the judgment. Second, Defendant appealed an order dated June 23, 2015, and the court is uncertain regarding which June 23, 2015, order was appealed and whether that appeal affects this motion. If the appeal is related to this motion then the court lacks jurisdiction per CCP section 916(a). The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aimee R. Morris, Esq., Law Offices, 619-991-0548, for defendant and moving party
Ansel D. Kinney, Esq., Law Offices, 415-421-8313, for plaintiff and opposing party

The parties agreed to conduct the hearing without a court reporter.

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PETER BENNETT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled with ten days leave to answer. The Marvin claim is adequately pled and the face of the complaint does not show that it is time-barred. The request for judicial notice submitted with the reply papers is denied as untimely because the request seeks to make a new argument not made in the moving papers and plaintiff lacked the opportunity to respond in writing to the request.
Defendant demurrer is overruled except Defendant is permitted to refile his Demurrer within 5 days or by December 5, 2011 on the limited issue of judicial notice as identified in the tentative ruling. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.
